svg.title: TrustSignal full high-level architecture diagram
svg.desc: Complete system architecture showing user inputs flowing through five detection stages (claim decomposition, NLI scoring with HuggingFace to Groq fallback, span extraction, drift detection, aggregation) producing a structured EvalResult, served via MCP server, FastAPI, or pip package.

g.onclick: sendPrompt('Explain how claim decomposition works in TrustSignal')
sendPrompt('Explain how the NLI scoring with HuggingFace fallback works')
sendPrompt('Explain how span extraction maps claims to character positions')
sendPrompt('Explain how drift detection works in TrustSignal')
sendPrompt('Explain how TrustSignal aggregates scores into a final verdict')
g.text: Combine NLI verdicts, spans, drift → final verdict
Notification hook: on_nli_notification(callback) — alerts on HF → Groq switch
Config: thresholds, provider keys, HF model — env vars or TrustSignalConfig()
Per-claim NLI labels + confidence + supporting source index

g.onclick: sendPrompt('Show me how to set up the TrustSignal MCP server')
sendPrompt('Show me how to set up the TrustSignal FastAPI server')
sendPrompt('Show me how to use TrustSignal as a Python library')

svg.text: All three surfaces call the same pipeline.evaluate() — identical output everywhere.
$0 total — Groq free tier + HuggingFace free tier, no GPU, no subscriptions.
